title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston police officers were shot at during a traffic stop in Dorchester; no injuries occurred, but the cruiser was damaged.
Suspect identified as Rasiel Joel Carbuccia was arrested and faces multiple charges, including assault and firearm violations.
The Boston Police Patrolmen’s Association urged officials to condemn violence against officers, emphasizing the need for their safety.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence was killed in a minivan crash on I-495 in Andover.
The driver of the minivan and three children were hospitalized after the crash with 'unknown injuries.'
The crash with another car, a sedan, occurred around 11:30 p.m.; the sedan's driver was not injured.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Three longtime Allston businesses, Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo, to close or move due to higher rents.
Alpha Management purchased 149-175 Harvard Ave. for $12.25 million, planning renovations and potential new leasing strategies.
Community concerns rise over potential changes in Allston's retail landscape and affordability following new development plans.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers selected Celtics legends Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett as his Hall of Fame enshrinement presenters.
Rivers will be inducted into the Hall of Fame as a coach with 1,194 career wins and one NBA championship.
The Hall of Fame ceremony, featuring the Class of 2026, will take place at Symphony Hall in Springfield on August 15.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum describes Jaylen Brown's trade from the Celtics to the 76ers as 'weird' during an ESPN appearance.
Jaylen Brown was traded to Philadelphia in exchange for Paul George and multiple draft picks, sparking a strong fan reaction.
Celtics fans expressed mixed emotions about the trade, with tributes and debates unfolding on social media.

title: How many first-round picks would Drake Maye be worth?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's Bill Barnwell values New England Patriots QB Drake Maye at six first-round picks due to his age and potential.
Maye, alongside Josh Allen and Patrick Mahomes, is ranked in ESPN's top tier of most valuable NFL players.
Notable QB trades include Deshaun Watson for three first-round picks and Eli Manning for Philip Rivers and draft capital.

title: Furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee is located in Gilford, N.H., and offers rental potential.
Built in 1952, the cottage has 1,037 square feet, three bedrooms, and direct lake access.
The listing price for the home is $889,500, with property taxes of $8,027 in 2025.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' features Matt Damon as Odysseus, focusing on human aspects over mythological elements.
The film employs a non-linear narrative, highlighting Odysseus's journey and personal struggles rather than legendary feats.
Released July 17, the movie showcases stunning IMAX visuals and a modernized take on Homer's epic tale.

title: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle just got married and celebrated at this North End restaurant
author: John Waller
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle married partner Devin during the All-Star break.
The newlyweds celebrated their marriage at Arya Trattoria in Boston's North End.
Tolle, 23, was drafted by the Red Sox in 2024 and has a 3.59 ERA in 18 starts.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Defensive Line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore lead the Patriots' defensive line, combining for 86 pressures last season.
The Patriots reduced their average rushing yards allowed to 101.7 per game, sixth-best in the NFL for 2025.
Khyiris Tonga departed in free agency, and Harold Landry is recovering from knee issues, impacting the line's depth.
